data.title: debug-everything: GitHub Actions pre-scan and mid-cycle bug list refresh
data.description:
## Context
Engineers and Claude sessions running /dso:debug-everything to restore project health currently must manually create bug tickets for GitHub Actions CI failures before the skill can discover and fix them. The skill also exits its bug-fix cycle without checking for tickets created mid-session — such as those generated by fix-bug's anti-pattern scan — leaving newly-discovered bugs for the next run. This gap means CI failures go unaddressed until someone notices them manually, and the skill under-performs on projects with active CI churn.

## Success Criteria
- On entry to debug-everything, a sub-agent reads debug.gha_workflows from dso-config.conf (comma-separated workflow file names, e.g. ci.yml,deploy.yml). For each listed workflow, it: (a) performs a pre-flight probe (bounded per_page=1 list call); if probe returns tool-not-found, emits "GHA scan unavailable: workflow run tools not registered" and exits; (b) fetches the first page of completed (non-pending, non-in-progress, non-queued, non-action_required, non-waiting) runs — if none found, logs "GHA scan: no completed run found for <workflow>" and skips; (c) checks whether an open bug ticket carries the tag gha:<workflow-file-name> — if yes, skips; otherwise creates a bug ticket with that tag and the workflow file name in the title. If debug.gha_workflows is absent or empty, logs "GHA scan skipped: no workflows configured" and continues.
- Between each fix batch in Bug-Fix Mode (regardless of debug.max_fix_validate_cycles setting), debug-everything re-runs the same scan and refreshes the open bug ticket list, creating tickets for newly-detected failures without matching tickets.
- The GHA scanning sub-agent returns only a compact summary to the orchestrator (workflows checked, new tickets created, failures already tracked); full MCP API response bodies do not appear in the orchestrator context. After each scan dispatch, the orchestrator writes the compact summary as a comment on the active epic ticket.
- When GitHub MCP workflow run tools are unavailable or return an error, the scan logs a distinguishable signal — "GHA scan unavailable: [reason]" vs "GHA scan complete: no failures" — and debug-everything continues normally.
- A config key debug.gha_scan_enabled (default true) allows disabling the scan entirely without affecting other debug-everything behavior.
- Running debug-everything with debug.gha_workflows configured and at least one failing workflow produces: (a) a new bug ticket tagged gha:<workflow-file-name> with the workflow file name in the title, and (b) a GHA scan summary line (with workflows_checked >= 1 and tickets_created >= 1) recorded as a comment on the active epic ticket, including the new ticket ID, summary line verbatim, and the workflow file name.

## Dependencies
None. Relies on existing sub-agent dispatch infrastructure, ticket CLI, and read-config.sh. GitHub MCP workflow run tools are a runtime precondition handled by graceful degradation. Known limitation: run-level CI status only; job-level failure attribution is a follow-on story.

## Approach
Add Step 0: GitHub Actions Pre-Scan to debug-everything's SKILL.md. The step dispatches a haiku sub-agent that reads debug.gha_workflows from dso-config.conf, performs a pre-flight tool probe (per_page=1 list call), then for each workflow uses GitHub MCP workflow run tools (list_workflow_runs_for_a_workflow or list_workflow_runs_for_a_repository) to fetch the most recent completed run (first page; no further pagination if none found on page 1). Deduplication uses tag matching (gha:<workflow-file-name>) as the primary check — deterministic and survives ticket title edits. After each scan dispatch, the orchestrator writes the returned compact summary as a comment on the active epic ticket. An identical dispatch is added between each fix batch in Bug-Fix Mode. Both dispatches are skipped when debug.gha_scan_enabled=false or debug.gha_workflows is empty.

## Scenario Analysis
- GHA scan sub-agent times out on large repos (high, runtime): 100+ workflow runs may hit tool-call budget or MCP response limits before diagnostic work begins
- Agent-judgment deduplication false negatives (high, runtime): Paraphrased titles may create duplicates — mitigated by tag-based primary deduplication
- MCP tool unavailability silent no-op (high, runtime): SC requires distinguishable "unavailable" vs "no failures" signal
- GITHUB_TOKEN absent in local environments (high, deployment): Fail-open path handles auth errors; config key provides opt-out
- Stale workflow run from wrong branch (medium, runtime): Most recent completed run per workflow may belong to a PR branch
- Mid-cycle refresh re-queues already-processed tickets (medium, runtime): Implementation must track processed ticket IDs within session
- Empty completed runs on fresh repo (medium, deployment): Sub-agent must return clean "no failures" signal, not error
- Incomplete run-state filter (medium, deployment): States like queued, waiting, action_required must be excluded
- No repository-name config for forked setups (high, configuration): Sub-agent derives repo from git remote origin; fork setups may need explicit config
- Haiku context window for large payloads (medium, configuration): Sub-agent receives only most recent completed run per workflow, not full history
- max_fix_validate_cycles=0 skips mid-cycle refresh (medium, configuration): Mid-cycle refresh must run regardless of cycles setting

### Planning Intelligence Log

- **Web research (Step 2.6)**: not triggered
  - Bright-line conditions that fired: none
- **Scenario analysis (Step 2.75)**: triggered
  - Scenarios surviving blue team filter: 11
- **Practitioner-requested additional cycles**: none
- **Follow-on scrutiny (Step 0)**: not triggered
- **Feasibility resolution (Step 2.5)**: triggered — cycles: 2, gap: GitHub MCP workflow run tools absent in current environment; resolved by config-based workflow list (debug.gha_workflows) and pre-flight probe pattern
- **LLM-instruction signal (Step 5)**: triggered — keyword: skill file modifications
